reasoning: I voted for Modi in 2019 because I thought here is a man who does not need my vote to feel important, a strong leader who acts. But five years have passed, and my booth president still takes his cut, my boundary dispute is still there, and big announcements from Delhi — the temple, the CAA, the raids — they are theatre. They do not touch Akaipur. But I cannot go back to TMC after what happened with that booth president, and I have lived through Left rule for thirty years already. So today I vote BJP again, not because I believe it will fix my village, but because I have no better choice. I am trapped between anger and exhaustion.
primary_drivers: Inability to return to TMC after 2016-2019 experience
Deep disillusionment that no party actually fixes village-level problems
Inertia from 2019 break with Left and TMC, despite BJP's failure to deliver
